paper_title: Analytical and Field Methods for Sediment Analysis

methods.description: Grain size analysis of sediment samples to determine texture classes using a vibratory sieve shaker and laser particle analyzer.

steps.description: Grain size analysis of sediment samples was performed on a vibrating screen Fritsch Analysette 3 Pro Vibratory Sieve Shaker (Fritsch, Germany) up to a fraction < 90 microns.
steps.searchable_summary: perform grain size analysis using a vibratory sieve shaker

steps.description: The fraction < 90 µm was analyzed on an Analysette 22 laser particle analyzer (Fritsch, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze fraction < 90 microns using laser particle analyzer

methods.description: Analysis of the bulk chemical composition of sediments using an XRF-spectrometer.

steps.description: Bulk chemical composition of sediments was analyzed using an ARL-9900XP XRF-spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Basel, Switzerland).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze bulk chemical composition using XRF-spectrometer

methods.description: Determination of loss on ignition (LOI) values by igniting samples and evaluating weight loss.

steps.description: Loss on ignition (LOI) values were determined by igniting each sample at a temperature of 1000°C and evaluating the weight loss.
steps.searchable_summary: determine LOI by igniting samples and measuring weight loss

methods.description: Study of quartz grains for roundness and surface characteristics using microscopy and scanning electron microscopy.

steps.description: Quartz grains from a fraction of medium sand (0.25-0.5 mm) were studied under a CM0870-T binocular microscope (Altami, Russia).
steps.searchable_summary: study quartz grains using binocular microscope

steps.description: Investigation and photographing of grains in secondary electron imaging (SEI) mode were performed using a JSM-6510LV scanning electron microscope (JEOL, Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: photograph grains using scanning electron microscope

steps.description: The roundness of the grains was determined according to the scale suggested by L.B. Rukhin and a five-grade scale suggested by A.V. Khabakov.
steps.searchable_summary: determine grain roundness using established scales

steps.description: Roundness coefficients (Q) and the degree of surface dullness (Cm) were calculated for each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate roundness coefficients and surface dullness

methods.description: Feldspar-based infrared optically stimulated luminescence (IR-OSL) dating to determine the age of sediment samples.

steps.description: Feldspar-based infrared optically stimulated luminescence (IR-OSL) dating was performed at the Research Laboratory for Quaternary Geochronology (RLQG), Department of Geology, Tallinn University of Technology.
steps.searchable_summary: perform IR-OSL dating on sediment samples

methods.description: Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS) C14 dating of organic-rich material to determine the age of samples.

steps.description: AMS C14 dating of organic-rich material from the bottom part of TK2 section was performed at BINP SB RAS (Novosibirsk).
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS C14 dating on organic-rich material

steps.description: The radiocarbon dates were calibrated using the OxCal v4.3 software and the IntCal 13 calibration curve.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon dates using OxCal and IntCal 13

methods.description: Field expeditions for sampling and site description in the Tura-Pyshma interfluve region.

steps.description: Field expeditions included sequential examination of natural and anthropogenic outcrops, as well as route studies within the territory of the Tura-Pyshma interfluve.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field expeditions to examine outcrops and perform route studies

steps.description: Samples for sedimentological and chemical analysis were collected by intervals from stripped walls, accompanied by description and photographing.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from stripped walls for analysis

steps.description: One sample from section TK1 and two samples from section TK2 were collected by driving 50-cm-long thick opaque plastic tubes into cleaned walls of the quarry outcrops.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples using plastic tubes from quarry outcrops
